sanskrit:
अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः।
कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
aparaṃ bhavato janma paraṃ janma vivasvataḥ
katham etad vijānīyāṃ tvam ādau proktavān iti
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: Your birth is later, the birth of Vivasvān is earlier; how am I to understand that You proclaimed this in the beginning?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices a surface-level doubt about chronological credibility, acting as a proxy for skeptics who struggle to accept that the eternal can appear within time.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between linear-time narrative and claim of timeless authorship; healthy skepticism functioning as social placebo to elicit authoritative clarification.
trigger_scenario: A junior therapist questions how her seasoned supervisor could have trained the founder of their modality decades earlier, unaware the mentor is the originator incarnate.
anchor_text: Time-bound minds doubt the timeless; truth transcends chronology.
